records.question: Which browsers are compatible with accelerators used by clients that are exactly End user, ISP?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser ON browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id
JOIN web_client_accelerator ON accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = web_client_accelerator.id
WHERE web_client_accelerator.client = 'End user, ISP'
records.pred_sql: select browser.id from browser join accelerator_compatible_browser on browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id join Web_client_accelerator on accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = Web_client_accelerator.id where lower(Web_client_accelerator.Client) = 'end user, isp' limit 5
records.thought: To find browsers compatible with accelerators used by clients exactly 'End user, ISP', join the browser table with accelerator_compatible_browser and Web_client_accelerator filtering by the client field.

records.question: Which browsers have a market share of exactly 28.96?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
WHERE browser.market_share = 28.96
records.pred_sql: select id from browser where market_share = 28.96 limit 5
records.thought: Filter browsers with a market share exactly equal to 28.96 and select their ids with a limit of 5.

records.question: What browsers named Opera with a market share of 1.2 are compatible since 2009 or later with the fasTun accelerator that has a client of All, connection is Any, and an operating system containing Any?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser ON browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id
JOIN web_client_accelerator ON accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = web_client_accelerator.id
WHERE web_client_accelerator.operating_system LIKE '%Any%' AND web_client_accelerator.connection = 'Any' AND browser.name = 'Opera' AND browser.market_share = 1.2 AND web_client_accelerator.name = 'fasTun' AND accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year >= 2009 AND web_client_accelerator.client = 'All'
records.pred_sql: select browser.id from browser join accelerator_compatible_browser on browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id join Web_client_accelerator on accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = Web_client_accelerator.id where lower(browser.name) like '%opera%' and browser.market_share = 1.2 and accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year >= 2009 and lower(Web_client_accelerator.name) like '%fastun%' and Web_client_accelerator.Client = 'All' and Web_client_accelerator.Connection = 'Any' and lower(Web_client_accelerator.Operating_system) like '%any%' limit 5
records.thought: Filter browsers named Opera with market share 1.2, joined with accelerators named fasTun having client 'All', connection 'Any', and operating system containing 'Any', and compatibility since 2009 or later.

records.question: Which browsers named Firefox have a market share of at most 18.11 and are compatible with an accelerator?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser ON browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id
WHERE browser.name = 'Firefox' AND browser.market_share <= 18.11
records.pred_sql: select browser.id from browser join accelerator_compatible_browser on browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id where lower(browser.name) like '%firefox%' and browser.market_share <= 18.11 limit 5
records.thought: Filter browsers named Firefox with market share at most 18.11 and ensure they are compatible with an accelerator by joining with accelerator_compatible_browser.

records.question: Which browsers named Opera have a market share of exactly 1.2 and have been compatible since at most the year 2000?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser ON browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id
WHERE accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year <= 2000 AND browser.market_share = 1.2 AND browser.name = 'Opera'
records.pred_sql: select browser.id from browser join accelerator_compatible_browser on browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id where lower(browser.name) like '%opera%' and browser.market_share = 1.2 and accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year <= 2000 limit 5
records.thought: Filter browsers named 'Opera' with market share 1.2 and join with accelerator_compatible_browser to check compatibility since at most 2000.

records.question: Which browsers named Opera have been compatible with user clients having a connection up to 756kbit/s since at most the year 2000?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT browser.id FROM browser
JOIN accelerator_compatible_browser ON browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id
JOIN web_client_accelerator ON accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = web_client_accelerator.id
WHERE accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year <= 2000 AND browser.name = 'Opera' AND web_client_accelerator.client = 'user' AND web_client_accelerator.connection = 'up to 756kbit/s'
records.pred_sql: select browser.id from browser join accelerator_compatible_browser on browser.id = accelerator_compatible_browser.browser_id join Web_client_accelerator on accelerator_compatible_browser.accelerator_id = Web_client_accelerator.id where lower(browser.name) like '%opera%' and Web_client_accelerator.Connection <= 756 and accelerator_compatible_browser.compatible_since_year <= 2000 limit 5
records.thought: I need to find browsers named Opera that are compatible with accelerators having a connection up to 756kbit/s and compatibility since at most the year 2000. This requires joining browser, accelerator_compatible_browser, and Web_client_accelerator with the specified filters.
